title: Fine-Grained Dataset Challenges
concept: Fine-grained datasets require expert labeling, suffer from class imbalance, and are difficult to crowdsource.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main memory challenge when implementing raw bilinear pooling?

options.text: It creates huge output matrices due to the channel outer product.
It deletes the weights of the network.
It requires saving all images to GPU memory.

exercises.explain: Correct! Outer products of feature channels lead to high dimensionality, requiring compact approximations.

cards.title: Finding a specialist
cards.body: If you need a photo of a dog labeled, anyone on the street can help you. But if you need to know if a leaf shows 'Septoria leaf spot' vs. 'bacterial leaf streak', you need an expert plant pathologist. This makes labeling fine-grained data very expensive.

cards.title: Dataset Hurdles
cards.body:
Fine-grained datasets are uniquely challenging because:
1. Expert Annotation: Crowd-sourced labeling (like MTurk) fails due to lack of specialist knowledge.
2. Extreme Imbalance: Rare species or models are hard to find in the wild, leading to long-tailed distributions.

cards.title: A spelling bee in ancient Greek
cards.body: Imagine hosting a spelling bee where all the words are ancient Greek medical terms. You cannot just ask random volunteers to judge the spelling; you need university professors who study Greek etymology.

cards.title: CUB-200-2011 Birds Dataset
cards.body: The Caltech-UCSD Birds dataset contains 200 bird species. To help the model learn, annotations include not just the species label, but also 15 part locations (beak, wing, tail) and 312 binary attributes (e.g., 'has yellow belly').

cards.title: Web scraping is not clean enough
cards.body: You might think we can just scrape Google Images for 'Elegant Tern'. However, because people frequently mislabel visually similar images online, scraped datasets often contain up to 30% incorrect labels, ruining fine-grained training.

cards.title: Standard vs. Fine-Grained Labeling
cards.body: Comparing the complexity of annotating data:

right.text:
Expensive expert hourly rate
Label: 'Osteosarcoma stage 2'
Requires years of medical training

exercises.prompt: Why does web scraping often fail to create high-quality fine-grained datasets?
exercises.explain: Scraped web data contains high label noise because laypeople often misidentify visually similar species or models.

options.text: Because users online frequently mislabel visually similar sub-categories
Because web images do not have red, green, and blue channels
Because search engines block all deep learning downloads
Because JPG images cannot represent fine details

exercises.prompt: True or false: Crowdsourced workers without specialized training are highly effective at labeling fine-grained medical imaging datasets.
exercises.explain: False. Medical diagnosis requires specialized training; standard crowdsourced workers will make many incorrect labels.
Rare sub-categories have very few samples, creating severe class imbalance.
exercises.sentence: Fine-grained datasets often feature a long-tailed distribution, meaning many classes are extremely ___.

exercises.prompt: Which of these features is a specialized annotation found in fine-grained datasets like CUB-200?
exercises.explain: Fine-grained datasets often provide local part locations to help models train attention mechanisms.

options.text: Coordinates of multiple specific object parts (beak, wing, feet)
The width and height of the image in pixels
The camera model used to take the photo

exercises.prompt: You are leading an AI project to identify structural cracks in wind turbine blades from drone photos. You have 10,000 photos, but no labels. How should you set up the labeling pipeline?

options.text: Hire certified turbine inspectors to label the types of cracks, and potentially use active learning to label the most uncertain samples first.
Send the images to random online workers to label quickly for a low cost.
Use a pre-trained ImageNet model to automatically label the cracks without human review.
Skip labeling and train an unsupervised model on raw pixels.

exercises.explain: Exactly! Since identifying structural cracks requires expert engineering knowledge, laypeople would miss subtle faults, endangering safety. Certified inspectors are required. Active learning can optimize their expensive time. Fantastic job completing the Fine-Grained Classification unit!
